evals.prompt: Make a 2364x640 banner from this book cover (a flat solid-coloured cover like the 1984 Penguin edition).
evals.expected_output: A 2364x640 banner where the cover sits centred and sharp, the sides extended in the cover's solid edge colour with no visible seam, all text preserved.
assertions: Auto-selects solid_extend (A) because the side edges are near-solid (edge_std < 18)
Output is exactly 2364x640 and the cover's text is fully visible
QA runs in mode='edge' and passes (no seam, colour + brightness match the edge)

evals.prompt: Turn this painted/photographic book cover into a wide header (e.g. the Madame Bovary portrait cover).
evals.expected_output: A 2364x640 banner with a full-bleed blurred copy of the cover behind the sharp centred cover, looking designed, text preserved.
assertions: Auto-selects blurred_backdrop (B) because the edges are busy/photographic
QA runs in mode='whole' and passes (fill compared to the whole cover, not the edge)
The centred cover is sharp (unsharp finishing pass applied)

evals.prompt: Make a banner from this small 180x279 cover image.
evals.expected_output: Warns that the source is low-resolution and the centred subject will be soft, then proceeds (or asks) and still outputs exactly 2364x640.
assertions: Calls the low-res guard and warns the source is under ~500px tall before composing
